LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. NO PARTY CONTESTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS ADOPTED. THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM WOODSON AS FOLLOWS AND AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF RAMA MODY'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. MONETARY SANCTIONS CANNOT BE GRANTED BECAUSE THERE IS NO DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF SUCH SANCTIONS. NO SPECIFIC AUTHORITY IS PRESENTED FOR ISSUING TERMINATING SANCTIONS, AND THE MOVING PAPERS ARE UNCLEAR WHETHER TERMINATING SANCTIONS ARE SOUGHT FOR (1) VIOLATIONS OF PRIOR ORDERS OR (2) GENERALLY FOR ASSERTEDLY FALSE STATEMENTS IN FILINGS, AND IN THE LATER CASE, NO AUTHORITY IS PROVIDED FOR THE RELIEF SOUGHT. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

Mtn Terminating Sanctions; Req For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-25-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Igor Kryan, Tel. 415.574.7011, defendant appearing on his own behalf;
Ludmila Kryan, Tel. 415.574.7011, defendant appearing on her own behalf;
Stacy Cummings, Tel. 925.356.1877, defendant appearing on her own behalf.

The matter was discussed. No party contested the tentative ruling which was adopted. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Woodson as follows and as modified: PLAINTIFF RAMA MODY'S MOTION FOR Terminating Sanctions AND REQUEST For Judicial Notice is Denied. Monetary sanctions cannot be granted because there is no declaration in support of such sanctions. No specific authority is presented for issuing terminating sanctions, and the moving papers are unclear whether terminating sanctions are sought for (1) violations of prior orders or (2) generally for assertedly false statements in filings, and in the later case, no authority is provided for the relief sought. The court will prepare an order. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489
